title: Military Transition Assistance Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Military Transition Assistance Programs 3 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF MILITARY ONESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides phone and internet support service through the Department of Defense, offering assistance and resources to military personnel and their families on various issues. Services include counseling and information on personal issues, money matters, career issues, parenting, legal issues, elder care, relocation, violence, trauma, and transition to civilian life. Website Eligibility: Individuals who meet one or more of the following criteria: -Active duty service member of the Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, or Space Force and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Service members of the National Guard and reserve and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Coast Guard members who are activated as part of the Department of the Navy under Title 10 authority, as well as their immediate family members -Survivors who have not remarried and children of active-duty, National Guard, and reserve service members (regardless of activation status or cause of service member's death) -Medically discharged service members and their immediate family members currently enrolled in their service branch's wounded warrior or seriously ill and injured program -Retired service members and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date, including service members on the Temporary Disability Retirement List. Coast Guard veterans and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service -Discharged service members (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date. Coast Guard veterans (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service. -Designated Department of Defense (DOD) expeditionary civilians, as defined by Directive-type Memorandum (DTM)-17-004, “Department of Defense Expeditionary Civilian Workforce” during the 90 days prior to deployment and 180 days post-deployment. -Immediate family of designated DOD expeditionary civilians, including spouses, children and anyone who has legal responsibility for workforce member’s children, for the benefit of the children -Military academy cadets, students enrolled in one of the service academies for the Army, Navy or Air Force -Active-duty foreign military service members assigned to U.S. Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force and Space Force installations who are registered in DEERS. Foreign military service members who have DOD-issued CAC with MWR/commissary/exchange privileges. -Immediate family members of foreign military active-duty service members registered in Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System (DEERS). This includes spouse as defined by 10 USC Section 101 and children. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 342-9647 Visit Website More Details OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE SERVICE NAVIGATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides service navigation to connect veterans to support groups, housing resources, and apply for benefits. 2055 Union Avenue Suite 2 North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must be Veterans Affairs Grant Per Diem (VA-GPD) eligible and pass a criminal background check Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (541) 808-9025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VETERAN REINTEGRATION CENTERS VETERAN REINTEGRATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide range of psychosocial services and counseling to support a successful transition from military to civilian life. Services provided include individual and group counseling for veterans, service members, and their families; family counseling for military-related issues; bereavement counseling for families who experience an active duty death; military sexual trauma counseling and referral; and outreach and education through programs like the Post-Deployment Health Reassessment Program. They also provide substance abuse assessment and referral, employment assessment and referral, explanations and referrals for Veterans Benefits Administration benefits, and screening and referrals for medical issues such as Traumatic Brain Injury and depression. Website Eligibility: Veterans and their family members Hours: Varies by location (877) 927-8387 877-- 247 Visit Website More Details OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE SERVICE NAVIGATION
